This chapter gives an account of Judah and his family, and it is such an account that it seems amazing that all of Jacob's sons, our Lord should come from Judah. But God will show that his choice is by grace and not by merit, and that Christ came into the world to save sinners, even the worst. Also, that Christ’s worthiness comes from himself, and not from his ancestors. How little reason did the Jews, who were called by this Judah, have to boast as they did. What terrible examples does the Lord proclaim in his punishments, of his complete displeasure at sin! Let us seek grace from God to avoid every appearance of sin. And let that state of humility to which Jesus submitted when he came to take away sin by sacrificing himself, in appointing such people as those here recorded to be his ancestors, make the Redeemer dear to our hearts.
